what: Compute Variance Using Survey Package Methods — tc_compute_variance
title: Compute Variance Using Survey Package Methods — tc_compute_variance
text: Core variance calculation engine tidycreel estimators use. Provides unified interface multiple variance estimation methods.

title: Compute Variance Using Survey Package Methods — tc_compute_variance
code: tc_compute_variance(   design,   response,   method = "survey",   by = NULL,   conf_level = 0.95,   n_replicates = 1000,   calculate_deff = TRUE )

title: Compute Variance Using Survey Package Methods — tc_compute_variance
text: design Survey design object (svydesign svrepdesign) response Response variable (formula character) method Variance estimation method: "survey" Standard survey package variance (default) "svyrecvar" survey:::svyrecvar internals (accurate) "bootstrap" Bootstrap resampling variance "jackknife" Jackknife resampling variance "linearization" Taylor linearization ("survey") Optional grouping variables stratified estimation conf_level Confidence level intervals (default 0.95) n_replicates Number replicates bootstrap/jackknife (default 1000) calculate_deff Whether calculate design effects (default TRUE)

title: Compute Variance Using Survey Package Methods — tc_compute_variance
text: List variance estimation results: estimate Point estimate(s) variance Variance estimate(s) se Standard error(s) ci_lower Lower confidence limit(s) ci_upper Upper confidence limit(s) deff Design effect(s) (calculate_deff = TRUE) method Variance method used method_details Additional method-specific information conf_level Confidence level used

title: Compute Variance Using Survey Package Methods — tc_compute_variance
text: core variance calculation function used tidycreel estimators. provides unified interface multiple variance estimation approaches: Standard Methods: "survey": Uses survey package public API (svymean, svytotal, svyby) "linearization": Alias "survey" (Taylor linearization default) Survey Package Internals: "svyrecvar": Direct access survey:::svyrecvar maximum accuracy Resampling Methods: "bootstrap": Bootstrap variance estimation replicate weights "jackknife": Jackknife variance estimation Design Effects: calculate_deff = TRUE, function computes design effects (DEFF) measure impact survey design variance compared simple random sampling. DEFF values > 1 indicate design effects clustering, stratification, weighting.

title: Compute Variance Using Survey Package Methods — tc_compute_variance
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(survey) library(tidycreel)  # Create survey design design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, data = creel_counts, weights = ~1)  # Standard variance var_result <- tc_compute_variance(design, "anglers_count")  # Bootstrap variance var_boot <- tc_compute_variance(   design,   "anglers_count",   method = "bootstrap",   n_replicates = 2000 )  # Survey internals (most accurate) var_internals <- tc_compute_variance(   design,   "anglers_count",   method = "svyrecvar" )  # Grouped estimation var_by_strata <- tc_compute_variance(   design,   "anglers_count",   by = "stratum" ) } # }

title: Decompose Variance into Components — tc_decompose_variance
text: Decomposes total variance component sources creel survey estimates. Built-tidycreel estimators decompose_variance parameter.

title: Decompose Variance into Components — tc_decompose_variance
code: tc_decompose_variance(   design,   response,   cluster_vars = c("stratum"),   method = "anova",   conf_level = 0.95 )

title: Decompose Variance into Components — tc_decompose_variance
text: design Survey design object response Response variable (formula character) cluster_vars Character vector clustering variables order nesting (e.g., c("stratum", "day", "shift")) method Decomposition method: "anova" ANOVA-based decomposition (default) "mixed_model" Mixed model variance components "survey_weighted" Survey-weighted decomposition conf_level Confidence level component intervals (default 0.95)

title: Decompose Variance into Components — tc_decompose_variance
text: List class "tc_variance_decomp" containing: components Data frame variance components proportions Proportion total variance component intraclass_correlations ICC values design_effects Design effects component optimal_allocation Recommended sample allocation variance_ratios Ratios sample size planning method Decomposition method used

title: Decompose Variance into Components — tc_decompose_variance
text: nested creel survey structure: $$Y_{ijkl} = \mu + \alpha_i + \beta_{j()} + \gamma_{k(ij)} + \epsilon_{l(ijk)}$$ : \(\alpha_i\) = stratum effect (among-stratum variance) \(\beta_{j()}\) = day effect within stratum (among-day variance) \(\gamma_{k(ij)}\) = shift effect within day (among-shift variance) \(\epsilon_{l(ijk)}\) = residual (within-shift variance)

title: Decompose Variance into Components — tc_decompose_variance
text: Optimal Allocation: Determine optimal days vs counts per day Design Effects: Quantify clustering impact Sample Size Planning: Use variance ratios power analysis Efficiency Analysis: Identify largest variance sources

title: Decompose Variance into Components — tc_decompose_variance
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Decompose variance for effort estimate decomp <- tc_decompose_variance(   design = creel_design,   response = "effort_hours",   cluster_vars = c("stratum", "day") )  # View components print(decomp) decomp$components decomp$proportions  # Optimal allocation recommendations decomp$optimal_allocation } # }

title: tidycreel 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: Survey-first refactor: Estimation relies day-PSU designs as_day_svydesign() interview-level svydesign; legacy constructors (design_access, design_roving, design_repweights) removed. New effort estimators (survey-first): est_effort.instantaneous, est_effort.progressive, est_effort.aerial, est_effort.busroute_design design-based variance (supports svydesign svrepdesign). Aerial enhancements: visibility/calibration adjustments; optional post-stratification calibration via survey. estimate_effort(), estimate_cpue(), estimate_harvest() now error guidance new APIs. Replicate-weight helpers removed; use survey::.svrepdesign() day-PSU designs needed. Added “Survey Package Creel: Translator”. Added “Replicate Designs Creel Inference”. Updated “Getting Started”, “Effort (Survey-First)”, aerial examples use as_day_svydesign() new estimators. Docs/DevEx: Updated README, pkgdown navbar, AGENTS.md conventions; modernized CI workflows (R CMD check, lintr, pkgdown).

title: tidycreel 0.0.0.9000 (2025-08-22)
text: Added survey-first CPUE Catch estimators: est_cpue() (ratio--means default; mean--ratios option) est_catch() (totals via svytotal/svyby). Updated README Getting Started vignette CPUE/Catch examples; exported new functions; added minimal tests.
